title: Utility Shutoff Notice — How To Stop It In 2026
description: Facing a utility shutoff notice? See free government programs, personal loan options, and exact steps to stop disconnection.
author: EmergencyFundGuides.com
excerpt: Facing a utility shutoff notice? See free government programs, loan options, and exact steps to stop disconnection.

blocks.text: A utility shutoff notice sitting on your counter means you have days — sometimes fewer — before your power, gas, or water gets cut off. That's a real emergency, and the stress it causes is completely understandable.
The fastest options most people don't use first: calling 211 for emergency energy assistance referrals, requesting a 30-day hardship extension directly from your utility, and applying for LIHEAP.

items: LIHEAP can pay your utility bill directly — no repayment required.
Calling 211 connects you to local emergency energy programs.
Most utilities may grant a hardship payment extension if you call before the shutoff date.
Personal loans may fund in 1–3 business days, but not all applicants will qualify.

blocks.text: Free and Government Help for Utility Shutoffs
The single most important step is checking for free assistance before spending any money. LIHEAP is a federally funded program that pays utility companies directly on your behalf.

blocks.label: LIHEAP
blocks.text: Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program — a federal grant administered by states that pays heating and cooling bills directly to utility companies for qualifying households.

blocks.headline: Not sure which option fits your situation?
blocks.body: Our free tool analyzes your specific emergency in 90 seconds and shows every option that may be available.

items.q: What should I do the same day I get a utility shutoff notice?
items.short: Call 211 immediately and ask for emergency energy assistance.
items.a: Also call your utility's billing line and request a hardship extension before the shutoff date.

items.q: Do I qualify for LIHEAP if I work part-time?
items.short: Yes, LIHEAP is based on household income.
items.a: Part-time workers, gig workers, and benefit recipients may qualify depending on state limits.

blocks.title: Your Next Three Steps
blocks.text: Start by calling 211 today. Then call your utility directly and ask for a hardship extension. After that, compare assistance and loan options carefully before making a decision.
EmergencyFundGuides.com is not a lender and does not provide financial advice. Loan availability and terms vary by lender and state. Not all applicants will qualify.
